evals.prompt: How should I prepare for a Product Hunt launch? I have about a month.
evals.expected_output: Walks through the 6-week preparation timeline compressed into 4 weeks: build PH engagement, prepare assets (tagline, screenshots, video, first comment), build a 300-500 supporter list, choose Tuesday-Thursday launch day, and explains the 2026 algorithm favoring comment quality over upvote count.

assertions.name: asset_preparation
assertions.description: Covers key assets to prepare including at least tagline, screenshots or gallery images, and the first maker comment

assertions.name: supporter_list
assertions.description: Mentions building a supporter or audience list before launch day and advises against asking directly for upvotes

assertions.name: algorithm_awareness
assertions.description: Explains that the 2026 algorithm weights comment quality or genuine engagement more heavily than raw upvote counts

evals.prompt: Is Product Hunt worth it for SEO backlinks? How does it compare to other directories?
evals.expected_output: Explains PH has DR91 dofollow backlinks (the highest of any launch directory), it's free, and every launched product gets one regardless of ranking. Compares with Fazier (DR81), Uneed (DR74), and others. Notes that while PH has the strongest backlink, the traffic spike is temporary and conversion rates are modest (~3%).

assertions.name: dr91_backlink
assertions.description: States that Product Hunt provides a DR91 dofollow backlink and notes this is the highest or among the highest of any launch directory

assertions.name: comparison_with_others
assertions.description: Compares Product Hunt's backlink value with at least one other directory such as Fazier, Uneed, DevHunt, or Hacker News

assertions.name: realistic_expectations
assertions.description: Provides a balanced view noting either temporary traffic, modest conversion rates, or that PH works best as part of a multi-platform strategy rather than a standalone channel

evals.prompt: I launched on Product Hunt yesterday and got 150 upvotes but I'm ranked #8. Products with fewer upvotes are above me. What's going on?
evals.expected_output: Explains the 2026 algorithm changes: ranking is not purely based on upvote count. Comment quality, account age of voters, engagement patterns, and steady vs. spiked voting all factor in. Products with fewer upvotes but more genuine comments from established accounts can outrank higher-upvote products. Advises focusing on driving quality comments rather than more upvotes.

assertions.name: algorithm_explanation
assertions.description: Explains that Product Hunt ranking is not purely based on upvote count and mentions factors like comment quality, voter account age, or engagement patterns

assertions.name: manipulation_detection
assertions.description: Mentions that sudden vote spikes, new accounts, or manufactured engagement can be detected or penalized by the algorithm

assertions.name: actionable_advice
assertions.description: Provides specific advice on how to improve ranking, such as encouraging genuine comments, engaging with commenters, or focusing on quality over quantity
